title: Masters Week Arrives: Scheffler Stumbles, Rahm Surges, Schauffele Sneaks Up
excerpt: The 2026 Masters field is locked in. Scottie Scheffler limps to Augusta on shoddy iron play, Jon Rahm roars in on back-to-back LIV titles, and Xander Schauffele circles as the quiet threat.
body:
It’s Major Time

The azaleas are in bloom, the greens are firm and spotless, and the 2026 Masters kicks off tomorrow at Augusta National. But unlike recent years when Scottie Scheffler arrived as an untouchable favourite, this week feels genuinely wide open. The world No. 1 hasn’t played a competitive round in nearly a month. His approach game has stuttered. And suddenly, the field smells blood.

Tournament Dates
Apr 10–12
Favourite
Scheffler 5/1
Top Form Threat
Jon Rahm 10/1
Field Strength
Incredibly Open

Rahm’s Renaissance

Jon Rahm arrives at Augusta in the form of his life — at least, that’s what the Spaniard himself believes. Back-to-back titles at LIV Golf Hong Kong and South Africa (the latter a play-off over DeChambeau), combined with a solo fifth in Singapore and four runners-up finishes across five 2026 starts, point to a man whose all-around game is rounding into championship form. His approach play has ticked back to 2023 levels — when he last won a green jacket — gaining nearly a full stroke per round on approach. At 10/1, Rahm has to be the wisest ticket in the field.

The Scheffler Stumble

It’s not doom-and-gloom for the favourite. Scheffler remains a lethal iron player and won here as recently as 2024. But a month away from competition, combined with approach play that has cost him strokes against the field in three of his last four starts, suggests a mild rust is setting in. His shoddy ball-striking at Houston last week — where he nearly withdrew to prepare mentally — raised eyebrows. The firm, fast conditions forecast for the week will not be forgiving to his current form. At 5/1, his value has likely been overblown.

Schauffele’s Quiet Threat

Xander Schauffele occupies the interesting space between favourite and value. Five top-10 finishes in eight Masters starts. Three consecutive top-10s in 2026. Six strokes gained tee-to-green in three of his past four starts. He’s the anti-Scheffler in temperament — loose, free, and seemingly immune to the pressure Augusta exerts. At 18/1, Schauffele offers both trajectory and form. Many experts are backing him to go one better this week and finally claim the green jacket that has eluded him for too long.

Sunday Awaits

Cameron Young, the Players champion, arrives as a threat. Akshay Bhatia has won this season already and is ascending. Harris English offers value at 100/1. And Bryson DeChambeau, for all his dramatic flair, remains vulnerable to Augusta’s approach-play demands. But make no mistake: this week belongs to either the man rounding back into form, the favourite fighting rust, or the quiet operator who has never won here. Tee off is tomorrow at 3pm BST.
